text:
built a photography streak tracker for the 5.4M people in photography communities who talk about 'practicing every day' but have no accountability

you get a calendar. every day you shoot, you tap. streak keeps going.

that's it. no gear reviews. no algorithm. just your streak.

photography-streak.surge.sh

the best camera is the one you actually use every day

except nobody tracks whether they're actually using it every day

built the tool to fix that: photography-streak.surge.sh

made a streak tracker for beat producers

not features. not FL Studio tutorials. just:
- today you made a beat: yes or no
- how long is your streak

beat-making-streak.surge.sh

kanye made beats every day for years before anyone cared

the people who become producers aren't necessarily the most talented. they're the ones who showed up every day.

built the tool to track your daily production streak: beat-making-streak.surge.sh

learning music theory is easy to 'start' and almost impossible to keep doing consistently

made a streak tracker for exactly this. study once a day, tap, streak keeps going. miss a day, streak resets.

no courses. no lessons. just accountability: music-theory-streak.surge.sh

music theory is one of those things where if you practice daily you'll make real progress, but if you do it 3x a week when you feel like it, you'll restart the same beginner concepts forever

1/4

the problem isn't the material. youtube has free music theory content that would take years to exhaust.

the problem is the gap between 'i want to learn this' and 'i practice this daily'

2/4

people who become musicians aren't smarter. they just have a system that keeps them showing up.

a 30-day streak on music theory is worth more than 3 months of inconsistent attempts.

3/4

built music-theory-streak.surge.sh to track exactly this.

tap when you study. your streak stays alive. miss a day, it resets.

no premium tier. no email required. just the streak.

4/4

built a streak tracker for outfit design / fashion drawing

the 5M people in fashion communities who want to 'design more' but never build the daily habit

one button. did you design today? yes/no. streak goes up.

outfit-design-streak.surge.sh

fashion school teaches you nothing about consistency

the designers who break through aren't the most talented art school grads. they're the ones who sketched something every single day.

tracker for building that habit: outfit-design-streak.surge.sh

put together a comparison of the best lead generation tools in 2026

not a sponsored post. just: what actually works, what the pricing looks like, what the tradeoffs are.

best-lead-generation-tools.surge.sh

lead gen tools are confusing on purpose

every one claims to be 'the all-in-one solution' and buries the pricing 3 pages deep

wrote an honest comparison: best-lead-generation-tools.surge.sh

what's your current photography streak? (days in a row you've shot something)

be honest

producers: what's the longest beat-making streak you've ever had?

most people i ask say '3 days' or 'i don't track it'

learning music theory question: do you practice daily or only when you feel like it?

there's a big difference in outcomes and most people don't realize it until 2 years in
this is why habit trackers for creative work exist — photography-streak.surge.sh
notes: quote tweet on any 'practice makes perfect' photography post

text: built the daily beat practice tracker for exactly this: beat-making-streak.surge.sh

what's a skill you practice daily that most people only do 'when they feel like it'?

asking because i've been building habit trackers for niche communities and the pattern is the same everywhere — consistency matters more than talent, and nobody has a system to track it

shipped 4 new streak trackers this week for creative communities

photography / beat-making / music theory / outfit design

each one took <1 day to build. all sitting at $0 revenue. the pattern is interesting: massive communities (2-5M), massive expressed interest in 'daily practice,' zero existing tools

next step: figuring out which community actually converts to paid
